steps.prompt: Which word is the finite verb?

hints.present indicative: Present indicative would be 'vivit' — 'vivet' has the future marker -e-
hints.perfect: Perfect would be 'vixit' — 'vivet' is future tense
hints.subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'vivat' — 'vivet' is future indicative

hints.video, videre, vidi, visum: These are principal parts of 'video' (to see)
hints.vinco, vincere, vici, victum: These are principal parts of 'vinco' (to conquer)
hints.venio, venire, veni, ventum: These are principal parts of 'venio' (to come)

hints.nominative: Nominative of 'panis' is 'panis' — 'pane' is the ablative singular

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'panis' has genitive 'panis' (-is)
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension nouns have genitive in -i — 'panis' has genitive 'panis' (-is)
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -us — 'panis' has genitive 'panis' (-is)

hints.accusative: Accusative of 'homo' would be 'hominem' — 'homo' is the nominative subject
hints.ablative: Ablative of 'homo' would be 'homine' — 'homo' is nominative
hints.vocative: Vocative of 'homo' would also be 'homo' but here it is the subject of 'vivet'

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'hominis' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension nouns have genitive in -i — 'hominis' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -us — 'hominis' ends in -is

translation: Man shall not live by bread alone
